strengths: Demonstrated task recognition awareness by attempting the correct intracorporeal suturing setup
Maintained consistent instrument control throughout the session
weaknesses: Performed task3 (Ligating Loop) instead of the assigned task5 (Intracorporeal Suture)
Failed to verify task requirements before beginning the procedure

drills.name: Task verification protocol
drills.description: Before starting any procedure, spend 30 seconds identifying the correct materials and setup for the assigned task.

drills.name: Needle driving angle practice
drills.description: Load the needle at a stable driving angle and practice 10 passes through the Penrose drain before any knot tying.
drills.targets_weakness: Preparation for correct intracorporeal suturing technique.

drills.name: Double-then-single sequence drill
drills.description: Rehearse one surgeon's knot followed by two single throws without time pressure to master the task5 sequence.

progression_note: At the advanced level, task identification errors are critical failures that prevent skill assessment. Focus on procedural verification protocols before pursuing benchmark times.
